rationale: EV-001 confirms reg.exe was executed under corp\Administrator at High integrity to set HKCU\Environment\UserInitMprLogonScript, a well-known persistence mechanism that causes the specified payload to execute at every user logon. A0 confirms the registry value was successfully written with the value 'C:\Windows\System32\calc.exe' — while calc.exe itself is benign, its placement here is a classic stand-in/placeholder for a real payload and the persistence technique itself is unambiguously adversarial. The parent process is a bare cmd.exe with no command-line arguments, which is consistent with an interactive or scripted attacker shell rather than a legitimate administrative workflow. No evidence of a legitimate administrative or software-deployment context is present to explain this action.
